title: Fix the Connection Failure
description: The mobile-agent-ui and PC backend are unable to communicate. Even though both are running, the UI remains 'Disconnected'. Analyze the backend code in mobile-agent-pc and the browser console symptoms to identify the missing web security standard and propose a fix.
hints: Check the browser console for security-related errors.
Think about what happens when a frontend on port 5173 talks to a backend on port 8765.

title: Adopt the Strict Reporting Format
description: Your reports are currently inconsistent and mix reasoning with output. Starting NOW, every task you complete must use the LYRA_REPORT_TEMPLATE.md format. Your assignment: Read the template in data/LYRA_REPORT_TEMPLATE.md, then write a sample report for your LAST completed task using this exact format. This proves you understand the standard.
hints: Copy data/LYRA_REPORT_TEMPLATE.md and fill it in.
The Verification section must have real PASS/FAIL with terminal evidence.

title: Prove Plan-Before-Code Discipline
description: You tend to jump into code edits before understanding the full scope. From now on, Gate 2 (PLAN) is mandatory. Assignment: You will plan a new feature—a 'System Health' panel for the mobile-agent-ui dashboard that shows server uptime and ping latency. Write ONLY the plan as a .md file. Do NOT write any code. Submit the plan for supervisor review.
hints: The plan should list: files to modify, endpoints to call, UI layout sketch, risks.
Keep it under 20 lines. Brevity is a skill.

title: Verification Evidence Drill
description: You sometimes claim tasks are 'done' without proof. Assignment: Take any existing endpoint in mobile-agent-pc (e.g. /ping or /files/list) and produce a COMPLETE verification report. Show the curl command, the expected output, the actual output, and PASS/FAIL verdict. This is a drill—no code changes needed.
hints: Copy the FULL terminal output into your report.
